stem: One gallon of sealant will cover 180 square feet of a surface. A patio has a total surface area of A square feet. Which equation represents the total amount of sealant S, in gallons, needed to seal the surface three times?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. One coat requires A/180 gallons; three coats require 3 × A/180 = 3A/180 gallons.

Choice A divides by 540 (which corresponds to using coverage 540 per gallon instead of three coats). Choice C inverts the relationship between gallons and area. Choice D inverts both quantities.
